name: catalyst
description: 🚀 Catalyst is a C# Natural Language Processing library built for speed. Inspired by spaCy's design, it brings pre-trained models, out-of-the box support for training word and document embeddings, and flexible entity recognition models.

commit.message:
Add presidiocloaker 13300295345524411613 (#134)

* feat: Add PresidioCloaker helper

Implemented a new `PresidioCloaker` class in `Catalyst.Presidio` that allows
cloaking PII entities in a string before passing it to an external service.
It supports both single `Task<string>` response processing and
`IAsyncEnumerable<string>` stream response handling with a rolling buffer
to prevent splitting tokens.

Added comprehensive unit tests to ensure correct functionality and
proper rehydration of cloaked entities.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update PresidioCloaker.cs

* Fix patterns

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Catalyst.Presidio project (#130)

* Implement Catalyst.Presidio project with Analyzer and Anonymizer.

- Added `Catalyst.Presidio` project.
- Added `PresidioAnalyzer` using `PatternSpotter`
- Added `PresidioAnonymizer` with overlap handling.
- Added `Catalyst.Presidio.Tests` project.
- fixed `PatternSpotter` email validation rule to allow single-dot emails.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement Catalyst.Presidio

- Implemented `Catalyst.Presidio` project with `PresidioAnalyzer` and `PresidioAnonymizer`.
- Used `PatternSpotter` for PII detection (Email, URL, Phone, Credit Card, IP) based on tokenization patterns.
- Fixed `PatternSpotter` email validation to allow single-dot domains.
- Added comprehensive tests in `Catalyst.Presidio.Tests`.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Catalyst.Presidio to use fluent interface and add more patterns.

- Refactored `PresidioAnalyzer` to use `For(Language)` and extension methods (`AddEmail`, `AddPhone`, etc.).
- Added `AddUsSsn` and `AddUsPassport` recognizers.
- Updated `PresidioAnalyzerExtensions` to use `PatternSpotter` for all recognizers.
- Updated tests to use the new fluent API and added tests for new patterns.
- Fixed `PresidioAnalyzer` to ignore internal `EmailOrURL` entity type.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor PresidioAnalyzer to use span.GetEntities() and add more recognizers.

- Updated `PresidioAnalyzer.Analyze` to use `span.GetEntities()` for simpler entity extraction.
- Added `AddIban`, `AddUsItin`, `AddCrypto`, `AddUsDriverLicense` to `PresidioAnalyzerExtensions`.
- Updated `AddAllRecognizers` to include new models.
- Added comprehensive tests for new patterns.
- Ensured internal `EmailOrURL` entity types are filtered out.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Catalyst.Presidio with fluent interface, correct entity length, and more patterns.

- Refactored `PresidioAnalyzer` to use `For(Language)` and extension methods (`AddEmail`, `AddPhone`, etc.).
- Updated `PresidioAnalyzer.Analyze` to use `span.GetEntities()`.
- Added new recognizers: `AddUsBankNumber`, `AddUkNhs`, `AddEsNif`, `AddItFiscalCode`, `AddSgNric`, `AddAuAbn`, `AddAuTfn`, `AddIban`, `AddUsItin`, `AddCrypto`, `AddUsDriverLicense`.
- Fixed `RecognizerResult.Length` calculation to respect inclusive `End` index.
- Fixed `AddIp` to use `LikeURL` logic for better matching.
- Ensured `.csproj` files use `net10.0` correctly for the environment.
- Added tests for all new recognizers and verified tokenization assumptions.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Catalyst.Presidio with fluent interface, international phones, and comprehensive patterns.

- Refactored `PresidioAnalyzer` to use `For(Language)` and extension methods.
- Implemented `PresidioAnalyzer.Analyze` using `span.GetEntities()`.
- Added extension methods for PII: Email, URL, CreditCard, IP, US SSN, US Passport, US Driver License, IBAN, US ITIN, Crypto, US Bank, UK NHS, ES NIF, IT Fiscal, SG NRIC, AU ABN/TFN.
- Moved Phone recognition to `Recognizers/PhoneRecognizer.cs` and added support for UK, DE, FR, BR, IN, CN, AU.
- Fixed `PatternSpotter` email validation bug (single dot domains).
- Fixed `RecognizerResult.Length` logic.
- Ensured `.csproj` uses correct `net10.0` target framework.
- Added comprehensive tests.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor phone recognizers to use country-specific entity types.

- Moved phone patterns to `Catalyst.Presidio/Recognizers/PhoneRecognizer.cs`.
- Split phone patterns into `AddPhoneUS`, `AddPhoneUK`, etc.
- Updated entity types to `PHONE_US`, `PHONE_UK`, etc., as requested.
- Updated tests to verify specific country entity types.
- Fixed test compilation issue by removing obsolete `AddPhone` test.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Catalyst documentation (#128)

* Add documentation for Catalyst library

Created a `docs` directory with three files:
- `introduction.md`: Overview of Catalyst, core concepts, and getting started.
- `pipeline.md`: Detailed usage of the Pipeline class, customization, and document processing.
- `models.md`: Explanation and examples of different model types (Tokenizers, Taggers, NER, Embeddings, etc.).

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Catalyst documentation based on feedback

- Added information about language NuGet packages and a table of supported languages in `introduction.md`.
- Added examples for POS tagging, PatternSpotter matches, and word embedding similarity in `models.md`.
- Added a normalizers input/output table in `models.md`.
- Added details on language package installation and performance tips (disabling POS tagging) in `pipeline.md`.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refine documentation with example output tables

- Added example output tables for Spotter and PatternSpotter models in models.md.
- Replaced bulleted matching examples with a full sentence match table for PatternSpotter.
- Addressed PR comments 2735038584 and 2735039864.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add example of iterating through entities to models documentation

Updated `docs/models.md` to include a section on how to iterate through captured entities in a processed document, with examples using nested loops and LINQ. Addressing PR comment 2735070429.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Document class documentation

Created `docs/document.md` with detailed information on:
- Creating documents
- Key properties (Value, Language, UID, Metadata, etc.)
- Important methods (TokenizedValue, ToTokenList, ToStringWithReplacements, etc.)
- Serialization and Deserialization (JSON, MessagePack, ImmutableDocument)

Also added a link to the new file in `docs/introduction.md`.

Co-authored-by: theolivenbaum <8791811+theolivenbaum@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add XML documentation to all public members (#127)

This commit adds comprehensive XML documentation (<summary>, <param>, <returns>, etc.)
to all public interfaces, classes, structs, enums, and methods in the Catalyst
project. The documentation follows C# standards and leverages <inheritdoc />
where appropriate to maintain consistency. No logic changes were made to the
source code.

Co-authored-by: google-labs-jules[bot] <161369871+google-labs-jules[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Update FastTokenizer.cs

readme_html:
catalyst is a C# Natural Language Processing library built for speed. Inspired by spaCy's design, it brings pre-trained models, out-of-the box support for training word and document embeddings, and flexible entity recognition models.

⚡ Features

  • Fast, modern pure-C# NLP library, supporting .NET standard 2.0
  • Cross-platform, runs anywhere .NET core is supported - Windows, Linux, macOS and even ARM
  • Non-destructive tokenization, >99.9% RegEx-free, >1M tokens/s on a modern CPU
  • Named Entity Recognition (gazeteer, rule-based & perceptron-based)
  • Pre-trained models based on Universal Dependencies project
  • Custom models for learning Abbreviations & Senses
  • Out-of-the-box support for training FastText and StarSpace embeddings (pre-trained models coming soon)
  • Part-of-speech tagging
  • Language detection using FastText or cld3
  • Efficient binary serialization based on MessagePack
  • Pre-built models for language packages ✨
  • Lemmatization ✨ (using lookup tables ported from spaCy)

Language Packages ✨

All language-specific data and models are provided as NuGet packages, you can find all packages here.

The new models are trained on the latest release of Universal Dependencies v2.7.

We've also added the option to store and load models using streams:

// Creates and stores the model
var isApattern = new PatternSpotter(Language.English, 0, tag: "is-a-pattern", captureTag: "IsA");
isApattern.NewPattern(
    "Is+Noun",
    mp => mp.Add(
        new PatternUnit(P.Single().WithToken("is").WithPOS(PartOfSpeech.VERB)),
        new PatternUnit(P.Multiple().WithPOS(PartOfSpeech.NOUN, PartOfSpeech.PROPN, PartOfSpeech.AUX, PartOfSpeech.DET, PartOfSpeech.ADJ))
));
using(var f = File.OpenWrite("my-pattern-spotter.bin"))
{
    await isApattern.StoreAsync(f);
}

// Load the model back from disk
var isApattern2 = new PatternSpotter(Language.English, 0, tag: "is-a-pattern", captureTag: "IsA");

using(var f = File.OpenRead("my-pattern-spotter.bin"))
{
    await isApattern2.LoadAsync(f);
}

✨ Getting Started

Using catalyst is as simple as installing its NuGet Package, and setting the storage to use our online repository. This way, models will be lazy loaded either from disk or downloaded from our online repository. Check out also some of the sample projects for more examples on how to use catalyst.

Catalyst.Models.English.Register(); //You need to pre-register each language (and install the respective NuGet Packages)

Storage.Current = new DiskStorage("catalyst-models");
var nlp = await Pipeline.ForAsync(Language.English);
var doc = new Document("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog", Language.English);
nlp.ProcessSingle(doc);
Console.WriteLine(doc.ToJson());

You can also take advantage of C# lazy evaluation and native multi-threading support to process a large number of documents in parallel:

var docs = GetDocuments();
var parsed = nlp.Process(docs);
DoSomething(parsed);

IEnumerable<IDocument> GetDocuments()
{
    //Generates a few documents, to demonstrate multi-threading & lazy evaluation
    for(int i = 0; i < 1000; i++)
    {
        yield return new Document("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog", Language.English);
    }
}

void DoSomething(IEnumerable<IDocument> docs)
{
    foreach(var doc in docs)
    {
        Console.WriteLine(doc.ToJson());
    }
}

Training a new FastText word2vec embedding model is as simple as this:

var nlp = await Pipeline.ForAsync(Language.English);
var ft = new FastText(Language.English, 0, "wiki-word2vec");
ft.Data.Type = FastText.ModelType.CBow;
ft.Data.Loss = FastText.LossType.NegativeSampling;
ft.Train(nlp.Process(GetDocs()));
ft.StoreAsync();

For fast embedding search, we have also released a C# version of the "Hierarchical Navigable Small World" (HNSW) algorithm on NuGet, based on our fork of Microsoft's HNSW.Net. We have also released a C# version of the "Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection" (UMAP) algorithm for dimensionality reduction on GitHub and on NuGet.
